calendar.matters:
MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Deposition Of Dr. Christian Foglar And Production Of Documents; Request For Monetary Sanctions; And Request For Stay Of Arbitration
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Off Calendar

Petitioner Geico Insurance Companys Notice Of Date And Place Of Hearing For Its Petition To Compel Edylwise Romeros Deposition And Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. THOMAS J. MURRAY, Esq. of Kern Segal & Murray (Tel: 415-474-1900) for petitioner Geico Insurance Company;
Atty. BREANNA K. JONES, Esq. of Covington & Burling LLP (Tel: 415-591-6000) for respondent Edylwise Romero.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER GEICO INSURANCE COMPANY's Petition To Compel Edylwise Romeros Deposition And Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted in its entirety. Petitioner Geico Insurance Company's petition to compel deposition and production of documents and request for monetary sanctions is denied. "Once a dispute is submitted to arbitration, the [California Arbitration Act] contemplates limited, if any, judicial involvement." (Briggs v. Resolution Remedies (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1400.) In Briggs, an uninsured motorist arbitration, the court held that "the trial court lacked the authority to review a discretionary, prehearing order of an arbitrator." (Id. at 1397.) As it explained, "the trial court conducted what amounted to a de novo review of an arbitrator's interlocutory order, something it had no statutory authority to review for any reason." (Id. at 1401.) Precisely the same conclusion follows here: the arbitrator has already ruled on the proper scope of discovery in the pending arbitration proceedings under the rules to which the parties agreed, and this Court "may not step into a case submitted to arbitration and tell the arbitrator what to do and when to do it." (Titan/Value Equities Group, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 482, 487-489.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
